ideation: This animation sequence depicts a person bouncing a ball.  The primary action is the rhythmic up-and-down movement of the arms, mimicking the act of bouncing. The right arm will primarily be used for the 'bounce' action while the left arm will provide a counterbalance. The animation will feature a three-beat rhythm to reflect the ball's bounce.  The joints will work together to create a natural, fluid arc in the arm movement.  The initial frame will show both arms in a relaxed position, slightly bent at the 'elbows' (using joint2). The second frame will show the right arm raised and slightly forward, as if about to throw the ball. The third frame will show the arm lowered, as if the ball has hit the ground. The fourth frame will mirror the second frame, but with the left arm now used for the bounce action, while the right arm moves to a relaxed position. This will be repeated in the fifth frame for the right arm.

frames.beat: right arm swings down to dribble the ball
right arm recoils, preparing for the next bounce
left arm takes over the dribble rhythm
right arm joins in, ready for a pass
